description: OWASP Top 10 for Large Language Model Applications

controls.name: Prompt Injection
controls.description: Manipulation of LLM behavior through malicious inputs, including direct injection (overriding system prompts) and indirect injection (through external data sources).

controls.name: Insecure Output Handling
controls.description: Failure to validate, sanitize, or handle LLM-generated outputs before passing to downstream systems or users.

controls.name: Training Data Poisoning
controls.description: Corruption or manipulation of training data to introduce vulnerabilities, backdoors, or biases into the model.

controls.name: Model Denial of Service
controls.description: Attacks that cause resource exhaustion, excessive costs, or service degradation through malicious or resource-intensive queries.

controls.name: Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
controls.description: Risks from third-party components including pre-trained models, training data, and plugins from untrusted sources.

controls.name: Sensitive Information Disclosure
controls.description: Unauthorized exposure of sensitive information through LLM responses, including PII, credentials, or proprietary data.

controls.name: Insecure Plugin Design
controls.description: Security vulnerabilities in LLM plugins, tools, or function calling that could allow unauthorized actions or data access.

controls.name: Excessive Agency
controls.description: Granting LLMs excessive autonomy, permissions, or functionality without appropriate oversight or controls.

controls.name: Overreliance
controls.description: Excessive trust in LLM outputs without appropriate verification, leading to misinformation or poor decisions.

controls.name: Model Theft
controls.description: Unauthorized extraction of model weights, training data, or proprietary information through various techniques.
